name: The White House
subject: First Lady Melania Trump launches ‘Be Best’
body:
Your 1600 Daily:
| | [](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=d5d3b7ac0c64527094ebce15394515162762315f19bc96c486c2eab8c626afe855f0df43acfd93de7e8fbac047e0b31e59a3053f706267dc) The White House • May 8, 2018
## The Day Ahead
President Donald J. Trump will deliver a major address on the Iran nuclear deal this afternoon. [Watch live at 2 p.m. ET.](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=d5d3b7ac0c645270d52a2d6ad8eb7f0846d59ced003fd43575cc497d10913727ebf55c13bd249190539a3e824c2c16443e929ff2ca86c530) |
---
|

## First Lady Melania Trump launches ‘Be Best’
**“It remains our generation’s moral imperative** to take responsibility and help our children manage the many issues they are facing today,” First Lady Melania Trump says. In an age of unprecedented access to everything from technology to prescription drugs, these challenges are more widespread—and addictive—than ever before.
**Yesterday, the First Lady announced “BE BEST,” an awareness campaign dedicated to protecting our children.** “It concerns me that in today’s fast-paced and ever-connected world, children can be less prepared to express or manage their emotions,” the First Lady said. “Children deserve every opportunity to enjoy their innocence.”
**BE BEST will include three main pillars** : well-being, social media, and opioid abuse. By promoting values such as healthy living, encouragement, kindness, and respect, adults can help children form positive habits early on and avoid turning to destructive behaviors as an outlet.
[Learn more about the First Lady’s initiative, BE BEST.](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=d5d3b7ac0c645270c2f4863cbbdea03428aa334617051e46ab27ca1d161202d53d744bd8aaa938dd73f662dd9dcada2d14e9a782b754c87d)
**Watch** : [The First Lady’s announcement from the Rose Garden. ](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=d5d3b7ac0c645270675de7e412ab5aa2efa61fd6697a0f3d17b92db6d9fb6bd7f894a7c0e9f08b9f04f1b2d2995eb5789bd4cd84a50f0086)

---
|

## Vice President Pence to Venezuelan regime: ‘Suspend this sham election’
**Vice President Mike Pence addressed a meeting of the Organization of American States** yesterday, calling on the regime of Nicolas Maduro to give the people of Venezuela a true voice in their government. “There will be no real election in Venezuela on May 20th, and the world knows it,” the Vice President said, adding that Maduro will resort to the “authoritarian playbook.”
**“Suspend this sham election**. Hold real elections. Give the people of Venezuela real choices,” the Vice President continued.
**President Trump first imposed sanctions on the Maduro dictatorship last year** , targeting the regime itself and not just individuals for the first time. Financial sanctions stopped the government from bankrolling its debt using U.S. banks. Yesterday, Vice President Pence announced that the U.S. has now also frozen the assets of three narcotics “kingpins” with direct ties to Maduro.
[Watch Vice President Pence’s speech condemning Venezuela’s sham election](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=d5d3b7ac0c645270ee3ce61e39d4f3b38e21406ff05fe72b02a923b38d04d49f0b4b4f11d5b6bb8c5330e0176bb69517830c6e36dd2c0e17) and learn more about the [aggressive sanctions President Trump imposed last year. ](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=d5d3b7ac0c64527066e2f11253a88aba4cfb36bf52d95e1a832e1c44d524190a3ccb3f6941106ccf7ae3f356d9f3ca073cc489148c3a4a37)

---
|

## Photo of the Day
Official White House Photo by Andrea Hanks
**President Donald J. Trump and First Lady Melania Trump at the announcement of the First Lady’s Be Best initiative** | May 7, 2018

[Privacy Policy](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=d5d3b7ac0c64527020a13b8b10f6f9f2f2985d9b29f21f6008316a8c60256c77e7172c976c86f0e77260b2920481519255c92d9fe5af5ee3) | [Contact the White House](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=d5d3b7ac0c64527013b87881a275d971c0d9466e4e6848c6ea5c922f20be9e69fd06285399a038ec063ae6df24091aae85c04b2a889529db) | [Unsubscribe](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/unsub_center.aspx?qs=d453c42a6560d1d4fe38da48d20025a480f269c7b8c160ffbc360ed3d5ceb22a5f6edf27cdd88581b80f9da3c749e454e4d1471ffa4f4fd6d3a458c4015e7d57)

| Friends -- The polls are now officially open! You've seen the ads, watched the debates, met the candidates as they traveled across the state...and now it's decision time.

The candidate you select today in the U.S. Senate race today will be the candidate who will beat Democrat Joe Donnelly in November -- and be a conservative, consistent voice in the U.S. Senate.

But the importance of voting doesn't stop at the top of the ballot. We have critical local races in communities throughout the state, and those candidates deserve your vote, too.

Republicans are exuding enthusiasm in this year's primary election, beating Democrats in turnout on early voting and absentee voting by more than 20%. Let's keep the momentum going!

The polls are open today from 6 a.m. to 6 p.m. local time today. To review your ballot and to see your polling location, please visit [IndianaVoters.com](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=104884f377&e=1acd9bb3d6).

name: Abigail Spanberger
subject: dark money against us
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Spanberger for Congress
body:
Things are getting interesting in the 7th District, Peter.

We just found out that a Super PAC is spending thousands of dollars to tip the scales against me in next month's primary election. This same group, by the way, recently spent over $400,000 in support of a Republican candidate in a Texas runoff. So we already know they have little concern for progressive values or for the needs of the 7th District.

I am not about to let them come into our community like this and influence our election.

We know _our campaign_ is the best positioned to beat Dave Brat. Our 1,160 registered volunteers know it. The 1,595 7th District voters who have donated to our campaign know it. And this Super PAC clearly knows it, since they're going to all this trouble. But as long as we continue spreading **our message of honesty, integrity, and service** to voters across the District, we cannot be stopped.

We're releasing a new ad soon, and I want to make sure we get it in front of as many people as possible. That means we need to raise another $10,000 by the end of the week, but it's worth it to ensure the people of the 7th are receiving an honest message. **[Will you help us by making a contribution today?](https://act.myngp.com/el/_XH4oUGirnTAoYhoh2NnCVvyHuH4NyX2gcuO9blLT5k=/55Ns7H2_md-L6r1K-Dt-LDiOxDRKOomT0mFVrp7G_M0=?refcode=super_pac)**
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/_XH4oUGirnTAoYhoh2NnCVvyHuH4NyX2gcuO9blLT5k=/yt9FeZRBd-S_L-07KVYCLEHj2oFntl3WppH93_XgP44=?refcode=super_pac)
Thank you. For everything.

Sincerely,

Abigail

[](https://act.myngp.com/el/_XH4oUGirnTAoYhoh2NnCVvyHuH4NyX2gcuO9blLT5k=/NSJs1W-6buKz8wqvLCVzayLe_5xWlhHjZNmWASdJjs0=)

_Abigail Spanberger is a proud Democrat and former CIA officer running for Congress against Dave Brat in Virginia 's 7th Congressional District. She believes in leadership that flows from our shared American values of integrity, responsibility, and decency, and she wants to work to move this country forward together. To learn more, please visit [AbigailSpanberger.com](https://act.myngp.com/el/_XH4oUGirnTAoYhoh2NnCVvyHuH4NyX2gcuO9blLT5k=/Zxg0VIs-20jWQHsTtKpbkxUw2340sp1ICy5Gs2Lg7Y8=)_

clean_body: Things are getting interesting in the 7th District, Peter. We just found out that a Super PAC is spending thousands of dollars to tip the scales against me in next month's primary election. This same group, by the way, recently spent over $400,000 in support of a Republican candidate in a Texas runoff. So we already know they have little concern for progressive values or for the needs of the 7th District. I am not about to let them come into our community like this and influence our election. We know our campaign is the best positioned to beat Dave Brat. Our 1,160 registered volunteers know it. The 1,595 7th District voters who have donated to our campaign know it. And this Super PAC clearly knows it, since they're going to all this trouble. But as long as we continue spreading our message of honesty, integrity, and service to voters across the District, we cannot be stopped. We're releasing a new ad soon, and I want to make sure we get it in front of as many people as possible. That means we need to raise another $10,000 by the end of the week, but it's worth it to ensure the people of the 7th are receiving an honest message. Thank you. For everything. Can you give me a moment to tell you why a monthly sustaining contribution is one of the best ways to elect Democrats, Derek?

By contributing monthly, you'll not only help candidates compete in critical races across the country this year -- you'll also provide the ongoing support it takes to build our party for the long haul.

Until recently, Democrats haven't done a great job of making the investments we need for long-term success. That's changing now -- and you can be a part of it.

**Will you make a sustaining contribution to help elect Democrats this year and beyond? Your renewed support will give Democrats critical resources to share our message with voters across the country.

** **[ DONATE: $3](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/405228bc/4067b0a/58eab1a5b/7e4401a7/1529145879/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/may-sustainer-2018?recurring=true&amount=3&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+DONATE+3&utm_campaign=em_20180508_ke_nd&source=em_20180508_ke_nd&refcode=em_20180508_ke_nd")**

There's never been a more important time to invest in this party, Derek, and there's no substitute for investing early.

**[You've donated to support Democrats in the past -- and right now I'm asking you to step up again. Make a $3 monthly sustaining donation today.](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/405228bc/4067b0a/58eab1a5b/7e4401a4/1529145879/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/may-sustainer-2018?recurring=true&amount=3&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=2+-+Youve+donated+to+support+Democrats+in+th&utm_campaign=em_20180508_ke_nd&source=em_20180508_ke_nd&refcode=em_20180508_ke_nd")**

In solidarity,

Keith

name: Belinda Biafore
subject: Our teachers deserve so much more!
disclaimer_text: Paid for and authorized by the WV State Democratic Executive Committee (www
body:
Fellow Democrat,
_You always hear the saying, “not all heroes wear capes.” That’s exactly the case when it comes to West Virginia teachers and teachers all across the nation.

I have said it before, though it goes without saying, teachers teach more than just curriculum. For years and years, teachers have used money out of their own pockets and from their own bank accounts to pay for supplies for their classrooms. They buy their students food when they don’t have much to eat at home; they sit with students after school waiting for their parent to get home from their second job, and they are a constant shoulder to lean on and a support system for children who need it. They teach compassion and work ethic, and as they demonstrated earlier this year, how to stand up for yourself and what’s right.

During this year’s legislative session West Virginia teachers stood together for better wages and healthcare and in doing so secured a pay raise for all state employees. Their solidarity and perseverance for not only themselves but their coworkers, neighbors, and students, showed the rest of the nation what it truly means to be a West Virginian. Did I mention that the movement was spearheaded and dominated by women?

The groundbreaking movement for teachers in West Virginia started from the ground up with teachers that were fed up and tired of being taken advantage of. Now, teachers across the nation are striking and demanding better pay.

Throughout the name-calling, closed doors, and disrespectful remarks from the Republican leaders, our teachers kept going no matter what Republicans said to them or how often they ignored them.

Democrats stood hand in hand with our teachers and put forth plans to raise their pay and fund PEIA only to be denied by the Republican leadership. That didn’t stop us then and it won’t stop us now. Our teachers deserve way more than their victorious 5% pay raise. They deserve way more than we could ever give them, but Democrats vow to continue the fight alongside our teachers.

It’s National Teacher Appreciation week and Democrats appreciate our teachers, but simple appreciation is not enough. Our teachers deserve better classroom environments for our students, higher pay with better benefits, and the respect that is long overdue.

Appreciating our teachers is a good place to start, but appreciating them means paying them more and fixing PEIA, and it means fighting for them the way they fight for our children. We vow today and every single day moving forward to continue our fight alongside teachers.

When you see a teacher today, thank them. & GO VOTE!

**Belinda Biafore, Chair**_

Paid for and authorized by the WV State Democratic Executive Committee (www.wvdems.org). This communication is not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. Public employees may disregard. Contributions are not tax-deductible.

name: Paul Davis
subject: Happy Teacher Appreciation Day!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Paul Davis for Kansas
body:
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/btj3I2DsoO-g9L7HTNQL99l-iCxOD4J7Z9ruu09Kwg4=/bng9t2NtkFui0qfJMMN3dhV7VC-YH3rQfMeekJ8BpOw=?gpsid=11_36_366&ms=em18_180508_teache_adv_gq_ful__PD&refcode=em18_180508_teache_adv_gq_ful__PD&utm_campaign=em18_180508_teache_adv_gq_ful__PD&utm_medium=email&utm_source=pauda&fn=Peter&ln=&pc=66864&em=dpwillis67@gmail.com)
Dear Peter,

For years, Kansas has failed to fund our public schools and pay our teachers fairly -- it's the reason I ran for the State Legislature in 2003. But now, 15 years later, we're still having the same discussion.

Public education has been the cause of my career -- and today, on Teacher's Appreciation Day, it's more important than ever that we pay our teachers what they deserve.

[](https://act.myngp.com/el/btj3I2DsoO-g9L7HTNQL99l-iCxOD4J7Z9ruu09Kwg4=/bng9t2NtkFui0qfJMMN3dhV7VC-YH3rQfMeekJ8BpOw=?gpsid=11_36_366&ms=em18_180508_teache_adv_gq_ful__PD&refcode=em18_180508_teache_adv_gq_ful__PD&utm_campaign=em18_180508_teache_adv_gq_ful__PD&utm_medium=email&utm_source=pauda&fn=Peter&ln=&pc=66864&em=dpwillis67@gmail.com)
---

**[Join me and demand better for our teachers. Add your name now.](https://act.myngp.com/el/btj3I2DsoO-g9L7HTNQL99l-iCxOD4J7Z9ruu09Kwg4=/bng9t2NtkFui0qfJMMN3dhV7VC-YH3rQfMeekJ8BpOw=?gpsid=11_36_366&ms=em18_180508_teache_adv_gq_ful__PD&refcode=em18_180508_teache_adv_gq_ful__PD&utm_campaign=em18_180508_teache_adv_gq_ful__PD&utm_medium=email&utm_source=pauda&fn=Peter&ln=&pc=66864&em=dpwillis67@gmail.com)**

If you ask any teacher, they'll tell you that it's not about the money -- but that doesn't mean we should pay them unfairly for the hard work they do. Every single day, these hard-working Americans work to educate our kids and build a better future for this country, and they deserve better from all of us.

Whether I'm in the Kansas Capitol or the United States Capitol, I'll always fight for public education and fair pay for our teachers.

**[Add your name now to pledge to stand with me and stand up for our teachers.](https://act.myngp.com/el/btj3I2DsoO-g9L7HTNQL99l-iCxOD4J7Z9ruu09Kwg4=/bng9t2NtkFui0qfJMMN3dhV7VC-YH3rQfMeekJ8BpOw=?gpsid=11_36_366&ms=em18_180508_teache_adv_gq_ful__PD&refcode=em18_180508_teache_adv_gq_ful__PD&utm_campaign=em18_180508_teache_adv_gq_ful__PD&utm_medium=email&utm_source=pauda&fn=Peter&ln=&pc=66864&em=dpwillis67@gmail.com)**

Thank you,

Paul Davis

name: Linda Awbrey, Andrew Gillum for Governor
subject: Overlooked
body:
[](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb30a9/9e00690/44c2d53b/240216f5/1215627175/VEsH/ "https://andrewgillum.com/?utm_medium=email&utm_source=gillum&utm_content=1&utm_campaign=em18_180508_Edu_acq_gq_act__LA&source=em18_180508_Edu_acq_gq_act__LA")
Hello --

I first met Andrew when he was at Gainesville High School.

Even then, I knew Andrew was a natural leader with a big heart -- he just needed the right encouragement and a chance to succeed.

As educators, we help create a path for every child to succeed, no matter where they're from. But under Rick Scott, that has become increasingly harder to do.

**[Add your name now to demand better for the future of Florida's education.](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb30a9/9e00690/44c2d53b/240216f4/1215627175/VEsE/ "http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/s/gillum-education-teacher-week?gpsid=13_55_367&source=em18_180508_Edu_acq_gq_act__LA&utm_campaign=em18_180508_Edu_acq_gq_act__LA&utm_medium=email&utm_source=agil&utm_content=2+-+Add+your+name+now+to+demand+better+for+t")**

I've seen firsthand how Florida's public education system is struggling. Schools are underfunded -- especially those in minority communities -- and teachers are underpaid. We tell children that if they get an education, they can do anything. But then, the people in power turn around and put education on the bottom of their priority list, placing those very foundations for success and the future of our kids at risk.

But I have hope -- because Andrew's already making plans to turn things around to make sure teachers are treated with respect we deserve -- so we can get back to what we do best: educating.

Under Andrew's Fair Share for Florida's Future Plan, he will invest in rebuilding our public schools, pay teachers a minimum starting salary of $50,000, and invest in early childhood education programs. Andrew knows public education is the best path to equal opportunity and that, as a state, we need to do better for our youth.

**[So today, on Teacher Appreciation Day, join me and Andrew in thanking teachers all across the state and to demand better for future generations.](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb30a9/9e00690/44c2d53b/240216f7/1215627175/VEsF/ "http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/s/gillum-education-teacher-week?gpsid=13_55_367&source=em18_180508_Edu_acq_gq_act__LA&utm_campaign=em18_180508_Edu_acq_gq_act__LA&utm_medium=email&utm_source=agil&utm_content=3+-+So+today+on+Teacher+Appreciation+Day+joi")**

Thank you,

Linda Awbrey

[Donate](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb30a9/9e00690/44c2d53b/240216f6/1215627175/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ag-may-2018?gpsid=13_55_226&refcode=em18_180508_Edu_fnd_gq_act__LA&utm_campaign=em18_180508_Edu_fnd_gq_act__LA&utm_medium=email&utm_source=agil&utm_content=4+-+Donate")

We elect pro-choice Democratic women who will fight for women and families. | [ ](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c59e9/24c69fd9/1c61e5fa9/188c0b55/1928364752/VEsH/ "http://emilyslist.org/run-to-win?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=1+-+%0D%0A++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++&utm_campaign=WQENEQ20_digital_email_20180508_RtWNoVATrainingRR&source=WQENEQ20_digital_email_20180508_RtWNoVATrainingRR")

Peter,

If you were at all confused by the subject line on yesterday's email, we apologize!

We are holding the Run to Win 101 training at the Women's Summit in Herndon, Virginia (_not_ Manchester).

**You can see all the details and apply for the training here:**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/Run-To-Win-NoVA](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c59e9/24c69fd9/1c61e5fa9/188c0b56/1928364752/VEsE/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/s/20180413_email_RunToWinNOVA?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgRunToWinNoVA&utm_campaign=WQENEQ20_digital_email_20180508_RtWNoVATrainingRR&source=WQENEQ20_digital_email_20180508_RtWNoVATrainingRR")**

Hope to see you in Herndon,

\- the EMILY's List team

_\-----------Original Message----------
From: Mũthoni Wambu Kraal, EMILY's List
Sent: Mon, May 7, 2018 at 5:59 p.m.
Subject Line: [You're invited] Run to Win training in Manchester_
Peter,

Run to Win is coming to NoVA!

In just a few weeks, my team and I will be in Virginia to train women about the basics of running for office. And we want you to join us!

Run to Win 101 is a free, half-day introductory training for women thinking about running for office.

You don't need to be a declared candidate or even actively planning to run to attend. You just have to have an interest in learning the basics of being a candidate for state or local office.

**[Apply to attend our Run to Win 101 training in NoVA on Sunday, June 24th.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c59e9/24c69fd9/1c61e5fa9/188c0b57/1928364752/VEsF/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/s/20180413_email_RunToWinNOVA?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=3+-+Apply+to+attend+our+Run+to+Win+101+train&utm_campaign=WQENEQ20_digital_email_20180508_RtWNoVATrainingRR&source=WQENEQ20_digital_email_20180508_RtWNoVATrainingRR")**
**WHAT:**
Run to Win 101
A free, half-day training for pro-choice Democratic women thinking about running for elected office

**WHEN:**
Sunday, June 24th, 2018
9:00 a.m. -- 2:00 p.m.

**WHERE:**
Herndon, VA
[](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c59e9/24c69fd9/1c61e5fa9/188c0b50/1928364752/VEsC/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/s/20180413_email_RunToWinNOVA?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=4&utm_campaign=WQENEQ20_digital_email_20180508_RtWNoVATrainingRR&source=WQENEQ20_digital_email_20180508_RtWNoVATrainingRR")
We're excited to partner with Network NoVA's 2018 Women's Summit to give this training.

We'll guide you through the nuts and bolts of planning your run, teach you how to get your house in order, and get you ready to take the first steps toward running -- and winning.

Space is limited and seats are being offered on a first-come, first-served basis.

**Submit your application to attend Run to Win 101:**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/Run-To-Win-NoVA](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c59e9/24c69fd9/1c61e5fa9/188c0b51/1928364752/VEsD/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/s/20180413_email_RunToWinNOVA?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=5+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgRunToWinNoVA&utm_campaign=WQENEQ20_digital_email_20180508_RtWNoVATrainingRR&source=WQENEQ20_digital_email_20180508_RtWNoVATrainingRR")**

Hope to see you there,

Mũthoni Wambu Kraal
Vice President of National Outreach and Training, EMILY's List

P.S. Can't make this training but still interested in learning about running for office? We're holding another Run to Win 101 training on August 11th in Richmond, VA! Details to come soon.

| [ ](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c59e9/24c69fd9/1c61e5fa9/188c0b52/1928364752/VEsA/ "https://www.emilyslist.org/donate?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=6+-+%0D%0A++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++&utm_campaign=WQENEQ20_digital_email_20180508_RtWNoVATrainingRR&source=WQENEQ20_digital_email_20180508_RtWNoVATrainingRR")

name: The Sanders Institute
subject: 67 million Americans without voter registration
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE SANDERS INSTITUTE
body:
In the last election, there were over 224 million American citizens over the age of 18
#
Friend,
In the last election, there were over 224 million American citizens over the age of 18 in the United States, and yet only around 157 million were registered to vote. Even fewer actually voted.
For 6 weeks, the Sanders Institute and founding fellow Senator Nina Turner are looking at some of the glaring issues in our voting system. This week, we are addressing voter registration in the United States.
**[VOTER REGISTRATION»](https://sandersinstitute-ourrev.bsd.net/page/m/2ba7ab88/bed43eb/50817608/75fda963/2990887798/VEsH/ "https://www.sandersinstitute.com/blog/voter-registration")**
[](https://sandersinstitute-ourrev.bsd.net/page/m/2ba7ab88/bed43eb/50817608/75fda963/2990887798/VEsE/ "https://www.sandersinstitute.com/blog/voter-registration")
Democracy is not defined by allowing only those who can figure out how to navigate a complicated system to vote. Democracy is defined by allowing the whole eligible population to have a say in the government. It is time we embraced our American democracy.
Thanks for staying engaged,
The Sanders Institute Team

name: Jay Inslee
subject: When Republicans are honest...
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Inslee for Washington
body:
...they admit what we all know: Their policies are bad for the American people. | [](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd79441/2f5ea88d/718cff67/2cbcd888/999583574/VEsH/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/page/s/honest-republicans?utm_source=ema_2018-05-08_honest-republicans&source=ema_2018-05-08_honest-republicans&utm_campaign=honest-republicans&utm_medium=ema") | **_Americans will pay more for health care because of the Republican tax bill.
\- Trump's Former HHS Secretary, Tom Price_** **_There is "no evidence whatsoever" the tax law significantly helped American workers.
\- Senator Marco Rubio_**
---
Friend,
Usually, when you hear statements like this, they're coming from, well, me.
I've got to admit, I don't usually agree with Sen. Marco Rubio or Trump's former Secretary of the Department of Health and Human Services, Tom Price.
But in this case? They took the words right out of my mouth.
[**Add your name if you agree with these two Republicans (for once): It's time to be honest that their terrible agenda, from taxes to health care, is hurting the American people.**](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd79441/2f5ea88d/718cff67/2cbcd888/999583574/VEsE/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/page/s/honest-republicans?utm_source=ema_2018-05-08_honest-republicans&source=ema_2018-05-08_honest-republicans&utm_campaign=honest-republicans&utm_medium=ema")
Look, I couldn't have said it better than what Price said at the World Health Care Conference in D.C.: "There are many, and I'm one of them, who believes that [repealing the ACA's individual mandate] actually will harm the pool in the exchange market...and consequently that drives up the cost." Democrats knew that repealing the individual mandate while leaving the regulations in place was going to raise premiums. Now Price is admitting it too: The failed Republican scheme to eliminate the ACA is going to raise our health care costs.
[I AGREE »](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd79441/2f5ea88d/718cff67/2cbcd888/999583574/VEsF/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/page/s/honest-republicans?utm_source=ema_2018-05-08_honest-republicans&source=ema_2018-05-08_honest-republicans&utm_campaign=honest-republicans&utm_medium=ema")
---
And when Rubio told _The Economist_ magazine there is "no evidence whatsoever" the tax law significantly helped American workers? He's right: Companies used their massive tax breaks to buy back stocks and consolidate their wealth; very few of them actually raised salaries.
[I AGREE »](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd79441/2f5ea88d/718cff67/2cbcd888/999583574/VEsC/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/page/s/honest-republicans?utm_source=ema_2018-05-08_honest-republicans&source=ema_2018-05-08_honest-republicans&utm_campaign=honest-republicans&utm_medium=ema")
---
It is shocking when Republicans are this honest. But sometimes they can't help it when the obvious truths slip out, even the ones they're not supposed to say.
You and I know that the Republican agenda is bad for everyday Americans. But even the Republicans are now admitting it.
[I AGREE »](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd79441/2f5ea88d/718cff67/2cbcd888/999583574/VEsD/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/page/s/honest-republicans?utm_source=ema_2018-05-08_honest-republicans&source=ema_2018-05-08_honest-republicans&utm_campaign=honest-republicans&utm_medium=ema")
---
Very truly yours,
Jay Inslee

name: J.D. Scholten
subject: I haven’t—and I won’t...
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Scholten4Iowa Campaign Committee
body:
Peter,
When I first started this campaign to unseat Steve King, I was told that to beat him I would need to raise at least $2 million—$500,000 before the primary on June 5th, and another $1.5 million between the primary and Election Day in November. As someone who is disgusted by big money in politics, this was hard to swallow. But the reality is, that in order to beat Steve King who gets much of his money from corporate political action committees (PACs), fundraising has to be a big part of our efforts. I’m happy to say that we’re closing in on that $500,000 goal before the primary, and we’ve done it the right way, with grassroots support from almost 6,000 individual donors!
**We’re about $30,000 away from our primary goal, and I’m proud to say that I haven’t—and I won’t—accept one dime of corporate PAC money. Instead, we rely on grassroots support from people like you.** [**_Help us hit this important milestone with a contribution today!_**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/1178667/6533468/-13720786?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDEvMS82MTU4MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjNmIyNzUxYy0yMjUzLWU4MTEtODBjMy0wMDE1NWRhNzgwMTIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=crGfA63e3_DoYcvIsHxF4v3LC0WAFqmQhv3GIansjDg=)
I’ve said it before, but it’s worth repeating: When running for office, one question you hear constantly is, “What’s your #1 issue?” There are many issues that are major priorities for me: health care, the environment, and income inequality are three of the biggest, but when you look at it, these three issues and most of the things I stand for all have one thing in common: big money in politics is obstructing their progress. Big pharma and insurance companies lobby against reasonable drug prices and universal health care. Big oil lobbies against cleaner, renewable sources of energy. And the one percent continues to push for tax cuts, like the one passed this year that ultimately end up putting more money in the pockets of the most wealthy and hurting everyday working Americans. For these reasons, ultimately, campaign finance reform should be everyone’s #1 issue. **We absolutely need to get the influence of big money out of politics in order to finally get things done in Washington. **
Politicians are far too quick to make promises they can’t keep, but here’s what I will promise you: **my vote will never be for sale, I will fight to remove the influence of big money in Congress, and I will always put the needs of the people of this district first. **
Standing Tall for All,
J.D. Scholten

We can't let Trump and Pence give Mitch McConnell what he wants by kicking Joe -- the only strong, independent voice Hoosiers have in the Senate -- to the curb. If we hit our $5,000 goal, we'll be telling Trump, Pence, McConnell, and their big donor buddies that Indiana's Senate seat isn't for sale -- it belongs to Hoosiers, not their wealthy donors. Will you help us send that message, Peter? "" \-- Team Donnelly ""** | Paid for by Donnelly for Indiana P.O. Box 891, Indianapolis, IN 46206 ""

name: Sally Miller, Team Smith
subject: In honor of Mother's Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tina Smith for Minnesota
body:
We need to be doing more for our mothers. | [](https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/m/4bc48cd9/c1f7015/68829184/114343e2/2331357487/VEsH/ "https://www.tinaforminnesota.com/child-care?utm_source=ema_2018-05-08_mothers-day&source=ema_2018-05-08_mothers-day&utm_campaign=mothers-day&utm_medium=ema") Friend, This Sunday is Mother's Day, and while many are making brunch plans and grabbing cards, mothers across the country are struggling. A few months ago, Tina traveled around the state to hear from parents who were scrambling to find affordable care for their children while working toward higher education. One story from a young women stood out: She had her daughter at 20, and thought higher education wouldn't be an option -- until she found a child care program on her campus. It changed everything for her -- and we need more options like it. [**In honor of Mother's Day, we're redoubling our efforts to promote affordable, accessible child care: Add your name next to Tina's now to show your support.**](https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/m/4bc48cd9/c1f7015/68829184/114343e2/2331357487/VEsE/ "https://www.tinaforminnesota.com/child-care?utm_source=ema_2018-05-08_mothers-day&source=ema_2018-05-08_mothers-day&utm_campaign=mothers-day&utm_medium=ema") And the truth is, parents all around Minnesota are struggling to find affordable, accessible child care in their communities. Tina has heard from families who have to drive 50 miles to drop off their kids. Some families can't find affordable, quality child care at all. That's why Tina's committed to doing more for our parents, and why this Mother's Day means so much to her. [**It's Tina's first Mother's Day as a senator, and she's committed to fighting for parents -- add your name if you support quality, affordable child care right now.**](https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/m/4bc48cd9/c1f7015/68829184/114343e2/2331357487/VEsF/ "https://www.tinaforminnesota.com/child-care?utm_source=ema_2018-05-08_mothers-day&source=ema_2018-05-08_mothers-day&utm_campaign=mothers-day&utm_medium=ema") Thanks, Sally Miller

name: Ben Jealous
subject: Our best and brightest minds
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY FRIENDS OF BEN JEALOUS, JASON SMALL, TREASURER
body:
#
Friend,
I come from a family of educators. And because of that, I know how much our teachers do for our young people and how important it is to support them.
That's why I am appalled that so many teachers have to spend their hard-earned money on school supplies for their classrooms. And that they work tirelessly year-round to provide our kids with the best possible education for inadequate compensation.
**[That is why today on National Teacher Day, I am determined to encourage our brightest minds to pursue careers in public service and high demand professions that will have a lasting impact on generations to come. If you agree, chip in $5 to our campaign today.](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a60c/7ab0e2c4/7fbbc7d4/349a4a3b/1957478767/VEsH/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiT0hRMDUwODhBIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bj-st5-18?refcode=OHQ05088A")**
My grandma's grandfather was born a slave, died a state senator, and co-founded Virginia State University. Before that, he built the one-room schoolhouse that my grandmother was educated in with his friends and neighbors.
My grandmother taught in a public school in Southern Virginia and my parents met as public school teachers in Baltimore. I'm now at Princeton University teaching public policy in the Woodrow Wilson School.
When I'm governor, I want every educator, every parent, and every student to be able to sleep well at night knowing that we're doing everything we can to ensure their schools are fully-funded, that we're restoring teaching to a first-class profession, that everyone in those schools are paid a fair wage and rewarded for their hard work, and that all of our students get the resources they need to excel.
**[Our teachers have been left behind for too long. As governor, I will ensure our schools are fully funded so every young person has access to the very best education. That's why I'm asking: can you make a $5 contribution to my campaign, so we can fix our education system and give teachers the support they deserve?](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a60c/7ab0e2c4/7fbbc7d4/349a4a3b/1957478767/VEsE/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiT0hRMDUwODhBIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bj-st5-18?refcode=OHQ05088A")**
Onward,
Ben
****[CHIP IN](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a60c/7ab0e2c4/7fbbc7d4/349a4a3b/1957478767/VEsF/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiT0hRMDUwODhBIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bj-st5-18?refcode=OHQ05088A")****
